title: Rich Flaky Northern Irish Yautia Afternoon Tea Scones
description: A salty and flaky traditional Northern Irish dish featuring yautia, perfect for afternoon tea, made with ferment-jang for added depth of flavor, and nut-free for all to enjoy.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, baking powder, and salt.
Add the cold butter to the dry ingredients and use a pastry blender to work it into a coarse crumb.
Stir in the diced yautia, ferment-jang, and sugar.
In a small bowl, whisk together the heavy cream and egg.
Add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients and stir until the dough comes together in a shaggy mass.
Turn the dough out onto a floured surface and gently knead it a few times until it comes together.
Pat the dough into a circle that is about 1 inch thick.
Use a biscuit cutter to cut out scones.
Place the scones on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and bake for 18-20 minutes, or until golden brown.
